NVDA.reasoning: NVDA is NOT in a pre-breakout setup — the breakout already fired. The prior squeeze released and drove price from ~$164 to the 52-week high of $216.83 on 2026-04-27, a +32% move. The stock is now in a post-breakout consolidation between $208.20 support (15 tests, volume-confirmed) and $214.73 resistance (7 tests, hourly). Range width: $6.53. Daily Bollinger Band width is 0.2591 — WIDE, meaning volatility has already expanded; the move is done, not pending. No new squeeze is forming (wiki confirms: squeeze_off:true from prior run, no new squeeze detected). Volume is contracting: today's relative volume is 0.84x the 20-day average (daily) and 0.56x on hourly — well below the 1.5x requirement for breakout confirmation. Hourly OBV trend is down. ADX at 54.67 confirms a strong established trend, not a coiling pre-breakout condition. A NEW breakout setup WOULD emerge if price consolidates tightly near $214.73 for several days, BB width contracts, and then closes above $216.83 on 1.5x+ volume — measured move target would be $216.83 + $6.53 = ~$223.36. That setup does not exist today. Raising confidence only to 30 because there is a plausible scenario where the $208.20-$214.73 range resolves upward as a bull flag continuation, but it lacks the squeeze and volume preconditions this strategy requires.

4 of 5 strategy agents are bullish on NVDA; only swing_breakout is neutral (not bearish — it simply has no valid coiling setup because the squeeze already fired on the $164→$217 move). This is a buy-the-pullback-in-trend thesis, not a breakout or fade trade. The regime context is strongly supportive: ADX at 54.67 is one of the strongest trend readings in the market, daily EMA alignment is fully bullish (EMA10 204.98 > EMA21 197.47 > EMA50 190.15), and ROC across all timeframes (5d +3.33%, 10d +5.22%, 21d +26.69%) is accelerating. Price has pulled back 3.5% from the $216.83 52-week high to $209.25, resting near the hourly Fib 38.2% ($209.70) and volume-confirmed hourly support at $208.20 (15 tests). This is the textbook buy-the-dip-in-strong-trend setup. The primary catalyst is live: hyperscaler AI capex disclosure week (Alphabet, Meta, MSFT, Amazon Q1 earnings) is in-window, with each confirmed GPU spend increment a direct NVDA revenue signal. Macro regime is risk-on (S&P +9.3% in April, best month since 2020) driven by exactly the AI hardware thematic that benefits NVDA. The macro agent's asymmetric R/R is 3.5:1 (10.1% up / 2.9% down), well above the 2:1 minimum. The 4-agent consensus on the entry zone ($209.25–$210.00) and stop ($204.00–$206.50) is tight, with two agents independently confirming $205.30 as the critical Fib 61.8% stop level. Confidence is 74 (not higher) because: (1) RSI-21 at 81.52 is extended — Branch A mean-reversion criteria nearly fired; (2) hourly MACD histogram is negative (-0.59) and hourly OBV is trending down, confirming the pullback is still in progress; (3) relative volume is below average (0.84x daily, 0.56x hourly), meaning no fresh institutional buy confirmation yet — entry discipline at the $208.20–$210.00 zone is required.
NVDA.key_conflicts: The primary conflict is between swing_breakout (neutral, confidence 30) and the four bullish agents. This is a setup-filter disagreement, not a directional disagreement: breakout explicitly acknowledges the bull-flag continuation scenario but cannot assign a buy signal because the squeeze has already released (BBW 0.2591 is wide, volume is contracting, no new coiling detected). The four bullish agents are all playing the same post-breakout pullback continuation — the breakout agent just lacks the right tool for that regime. A secondary conflict exists on targets: macro_context projects $231.13 (the full Fib 1.272 extension of the $164–$217 major swing), while trend_momentum, mean_reversion, and catalyst_news cluster tightly at $221.89–$222.50 (the swing high retest plus modest extension). The $231.13 target is structurally valid for a longer holding period but is optimistic for a 5–13 day swing window. The synthesized target of $221.89 uses the cluster median and is the more defensible near-term objective. Stops are broadly consistent: trend_momentum and mean_reversion both pin $205.30 (hourly Fib 61.8%), catalyst_news uses $206.50 (above prev-day low), and macro uses $204.00 (daily EMA10 invalidation). The synthesized stop of $205.30 is the most commonly cited level and is structurally anchored at the Fib 61.8% retracement, which is the correct pullback invalidation point within a bull trend.

Fed held at 4.25-4.50% for third consecutive meeting (historic 8-4 dissent); zero cuts priced through 2026-2027. Higher-for-longer firmly in place. Fragile backdrop: Iran/Strait of Hormuz blockade elevating Brent toward $90/bbl, core PCE at ~2.7%, and Warsh succession adding policy uncertainty. The AI capex supercycle is the dominant risk-on driver and directly benefits NVDA — the regime wind is at this trade's back despite macro headwinds elsewhere. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: NVDA is a strong sector outperformer. 21d ROC at +26.69% vs S&P ~+9.3% monthly gain — nearly 3x the broad market. 5d ROC +3.33%, 10d ROC +5.22%. All EMAs fully aligned in uptrend (EMA5 through EMA200). ADX at 54.67 (strong trend) with +DI 39.03 vs -DI 4.66. Price holds above all moving averages. This is a relative-strength leader by a wide margin, consistent with institutional sector-rotation inflows into AI hardware. 3. ASYMMETRIC R/R: Entry ~$210.00 on stabilization above hourly volume-confirmed support $208.20. Target: $231.13 (daily Fib 1.272 extension from $164.27-$216.83 swing) = +10.1% upside. Stop: $204.00 (below daily EMA-10 at $204.98 and Fib 23.6% at $204.43, invalidating relative-strength thesis if broken) = -2.9% downside. CAPITAL PRESERVATION: NVDA balance sheet shows near-zero leverage per wiki thesis — no concern if conditions tighten. ATR-14 at $5.87 (2.81% daily) is elevated but regime-consistent for a high-beta AI name in risk-on. No leverage flag triggered. Hourly OBV is trending down and hourly MACD histogram is negative (-0.5885), suggesting near-term distribution/consolidation rather than fresh buying. Volume was below average on Apr 29 (0.84x). The 3-day pullback from $216.83 to $209.25 looks like a healthy pause in the uptrend rather than distribution, but entry discipline matters: wait for hourly stabilization above $208.20 support before committing. Wiki thesis confirms NVDA has been a consistent buy-on-pullback-to-EMA10 trade across 5 prior runs. Sector rotation cycle appears mid-stage — not late — given the fresh 26d momentum burst.
